$comment: Permission rules the kit has SHIPPED in the past and has since removed. On a layer-in-place upgrade, install.sh offers to retire any of these still present in a profile (default: drop = adopt the updated set), while leaving rules the kit never shipped (the user's own) untouched.

Maintenance contract: whenever you delete or change a deny/allow rule in settings.base.json or rtk-allowlist.json, append the OLD string here so existing profiles can drop it on their next upgrade. Never remove entries from this list — it is the kit's permission-rule history.

retiredAdditions: whole additions the kit shipped before and has since dropped from additions.json. Because the uninstall loop only sees ids still in the manifest, a fully-removed addition's files would orphan in existing profiles. List each one's owned paths (relative to the config dir) here and install.sh deletes them on upgrade. Skills/commands only — retired HOOKS self-clean via their managed marker. Never remove entries; this is the kit's retired-addition history.
